<!--
Thank you for offering a case study. The series rules are at
docs/case-studies/README.md — real uses told honestly, no composite
customers, numbers measured or none, recollection labeled as recollection.
Promotion from this issue to a published entry under docs/case-studies/
happens via a co-authored pull request, so your name lands in the commit
history alongside the maintainer's.

Each prompt below is short. A few sentences per section is enough to start
the conversation.
-->

## What was the problem

<!-- The shape of the situation before skill-engine showed up. Who was the work for, what was the constraint, what made it the kind of problem that wasn't going to yield to "just read more code". -->

## What you did with skill-engine

<!-- Which sources you registered, which workflow you ran (discover / refresh / self-audit / compose multiple contextualizers), and any composition with other tools or workflows. Mechanism specifics are welcome; identity specifics (employer, client, product, internal package names) are not — see the truth-contract. -->

## What changed

<!-- The artifact the engine produced and what the team or you did with it next. Be precise about where the engine's contribution stopped and where someone else's started. -->

## What you can prove (or honestly can't)

<!-- Anything measured at the time goes here. Anything you remember but didn't instrument is welcome too — label it as recollection. Reconstructed numbers are not a fit for this series. -->
